---
title: "nitrix"
subtitle: "GPU-accelerated pure numerics for neuroimaging in JAX/XLA"
toc: false
---

`nitrix` is the substrate numerics layer of the federation: statistics,
geometry, registration, signal, graph, and morphology, written as pure
JAX/XLA so they run — differentiably, batched, JIT-compiled — on a hardware
accelerator.

::: {.callout-tldr title="Fast path"}
New to GPUs, JAX/XLA, or automatic differentiation? Start with [the
primer](explanation/primer/index.qmd) — the foundations the rest of the book leans
on. Then three textbook units for readers who know linear algebra but not the
domain: [the statistical modelling suite](explanation/statistics/index.qmd) —
climbing from generalised linear models up to Gaussian processes — [discrete
geometry](explanation/geometry/index.qmd) — meshes, volumes, and the operators on
them — and [registration](explanation/registration/index.qmd) — the maps between
those geometries. For statistics, read the [GLM
foundation](explanation/statistics/glm.qmd) first ("every model is GLM plus one
idea"); for geometry, the [operator algebra](explanation/geometry/operators.qmd);
for registration, the [foundations](explanation/registration/foundations.qmd).
:::

## Explanation (the textbook layer)

- [The primer](explanation/primer/index.qmd) — the foundations the rest of the book
  leans on: programming the GPU, the JAX/XLA compiler and its levels of
  representation, the calculus of automatic differentiation, and the inductive
  biases that tie the units together.
  - [Programming the GPU](explanation/primer/hardware.qmd)
  - [JAX and XLA: levels of representation](explanation/primer/xla-jax.qmd)
- [The statistical modelling suite](explanation/statistics/index.qmd) — the model
  ladder (GLM → LME → GAM → GLMM → GAMM → HSGP) and the shared algorithms behind it.
  - [Generalised linear models: the foundation](explanation/statistics/glm.qmd)
  - [Choosing smoothing parameters: the Fellner–Schall method](explanation/statistics/fellner-schall.qmd)
- [Discrete geometry](explanation/geometry/index.qmd) — meshes, volumes, and the
  operators on them: the Laplace–Beltrami operator, curvature, morphology and
  distance transforms, isosurfaces, over a shared semiring substrate.
  - [The operator algebra: ELL + semirings](explanation/geometry/operators.qmd)
  - [The Laplace–Beltrami operator](explanation/geometry/laplace-beltrami.qmd)
- [Registration](explanation/registration/index.qmd) — maps between geometries:
  the transform ladder (rigid → affine → diffeomorphic → surface), similarity
  metrics, differentiable resampling, and the optimisation that finds the map.
  - [The registration problem](explanation/registration/foundations.qmd)
  - [Linear transforms as a Lie group](explanation/registration/transforms.qmd)

More units (signal, connectivity and labels) follow the same pattern: derive the
mathematics, justify the design, point at the code.
